title: "Error: chown /home/avi/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/<hash>/_data/conf: no such file or directory" trying to create a container with a volume, without mounting it
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Trying to create/run a container, I get a chown error. This happens even if I delete the image prior to running.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman run docker.io/scylladb/scylla-enterprise:2019.1.9


**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: chown /home/avi/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/<hash>/_data/conf
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Successful container start.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Adding `-v /var/lib/scylla:/var/lib/scylla` makes it work (satisfying the volume requirement with an external volume).

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 2.0.3
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

My poor long-suffering desktop.

comments.body:
What is the UID of the data directory?  Who is supposed to own it?
I think the issue is probably the UID is > 65k which is all of the UIDs allocated to your user account.

Not sure what you mean exactly by "the data directory".

```
$ ls -l /home/avi/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/22139df596034728e64904be5dd3f4798b948ee5d492c071f3d957605b033bfa/_data/conf
lrwxrwxrwx 1 100998 100999 11 Jun 11 13:59 /home/avi/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/22139df596034728e64904be5dd3f4798b948ee5d492c071f3d957605b033bfa/_data/conf -> /etc/scylla
```

So maybe the fact that a symlink is involved is relevant. Missing O_NOFOLLOW somewhere?

```
$ cat /etc/subuid
avi:100000:65536
distcc:200000:65536
jenkins:300000:65536
```

So in range.

Examining parent directories:

```
$ ls -ld /home/avi/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/22139df596034728e64904be5dd3f4798b948ee5d492c071f3d957605b033bfa/_data
drwxr-xr-x 7 avi avi 94 Jul 27 18:23 /home/avi/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/22139df596034728e64904be5dd3f4798b948ee5d492c071f3d957605b033bfa/_data
```
```
$ ls -ld /home/avi/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/22139df596034728e64904be5dd3f4798b948ee5d492c071f3d957605b033bfa/
drwx------ 3 avi avi 19 Jul 27 18:23 /home/avi/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/22139df596034728e64904be5dd3f4798b948ee5d492c071f3d957605b033bfa/
```
I misunderstood what is going on.  Ok so this looks like we are chowning a symbolic link to no where.
FYI, I hit the same issue, and can confirm that upgrading to `podman` 2.0.4 fixes the problem.
